steps.title: Build the margin interval
steps.content:
The estimate is $30\%$ with a margin of error of $3\%$. That gives the interval
$$30\%-3\%=27\%$$
$$30\%+3\%=33\%$$

steps.title: Interpret values outside it
steps.content: A margin of error does not make values outside the interval impossible, but it does make them doubtful. Choice A treats margin of error as a classification error in the sample, not uncertainty about the larger teen population. Choice B is too absolute, and Choice C names only the upper endpoint. Since $35\%$ is greater than the upper end, $33\%$, it is doubtful that the population percent is $35\%$. The answer is D.
